NAME

main.c - 🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

Number of loaded server identity keys. public_key_t g_client_whitelist [MAX_CLIENTS] = {0}
Global client public key whitelist. size_t g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0
Number of whitelisted clients.

Detailed Description

🖥️ Server main entry point: multi-client connection manager with per-client rendering threads (60fps video + 172fps audio)

MODULAR COMPONENTS:

  • main.c (this file): Server initialization, signal handling, connection management
  • client.c: Per-client lifecycle, threading, and state management
  • protocol.c: Network packet processing and protocol implementation
  • stream.c: Video mixing, ASCII frame generation, and caching
  • render.c: Per-client rendering threads with rate limiting
  • stats.c: Performance monitoring and resource tracking

CONCURRENCY MODEL:

The server creates multiple thread types per client:

1.
Receive thread: Handles incoming packets from client (protocol.c functions)
2.
Send thread: Manages outgoing packet delivery (client.c)
3.
Video render thread: Generates ASCII frames at 60fps (render.c)
4.
Audio render thread: Mixes audio streams at 172fps (render.c)
5.
Stats logger thread: Periodic performance reporting (stats.c)

Thread Safety:

  • Lock ordering: Always acquire g_client_manager_rwlock BEFORE per-client mutexes
  • Snapshot pattern: Copy client state under mutex, then process without locks
  • Signal-safe shutdown: SIGINT handler only sets flags and closes sockets
  • Deterministic cleanup: Main thread waits for all worker threads before exit

WHY THE REFACTORING:

The original server.c became unmaintainable at 2408+ lines, making it:

  • Too large for LLM context windows (limited AI-assisted development)
  • Difficult for humans to navigate and understand
  • Slow to compile and modify
  • Hard to isolate bugs and add new features

The modular design enables:

  • Faster development cycles (smaller compilation units)
  • Better IDE support (jump-to-definition, IntelliSense)
  • Easier testing and debugging (isolated components)
  • Future extensibility (new protocols, renderers, optimizations)

Author

Zachary Fogg me@zfo.gg

Date

September 2025

Version

2.0 (Post-Modularization)

Function Documentation

int server_main (void )

Server mode entry point for unified binary. This function implements the complete server lifecycle including:

  • Server-specific initialization (crypto, shutdown callback)
  • Network socket setup and binding
  • Main connection accept loop
  • Client lifecycle management
  • Graceful shutdown and cleanup

Options are already parsed by the main dispatcher before this function is called, so they are available via global opt_* variables.

Returns

0 on success, non-zero error code on failure

Example

# Invoked by dispatcher after options are parsed:
ascii-chat server --port 8080
# Options parsed in main.c, then server_main() called

Definition at line 1774 of file server/main.c.

1803   log_debug("Crypto initialized successfully");

1823   ascii_simd_init();

1829   // Handle Ctrl+C for cleanup

1861   // Network setup - Use tcp_server abstraction for dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 binding
1862   log_debug("Config check: GET_OPTION(address)='%s', GET_OPTION(address6)='%s'", GET_OPTION(address),
1863             GET_OPTION(address6));
1864 
1865   bool ipv4_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address)) > 0);
1866   bool ipv6_has_value = (strlen(GET_OPTION(address6)) > 0);
1867   // Check if address is localhost (any loopback address, not just exact default)
1868   bool ipv4_is_default = is_localhost_ipv4(GET_OPTION(address));
1869   bool ipv6_is_default = is_localhost_ipv6(GET_OPTION(address6));
1870 
1871   log_debug("Binding decision: ipv4_has_value=%d, ipv6_has_value=%d, ipv4_is_default=%d, ipv6_is_default=%d",
1872             ipv4_has_value, ipv6_has_value, ipv4_is_default, ipv6_is_default);
1873 
1874   // Determine bind configuration
1875   bool bind_ipv4 = false;
1876   bool bind_ipv6 = false;
1877   const char *ipv4_address = NULL;
1878   const char *ipv6_address = NULL;
1879 
1880   if (ipv4_has_value && ipv6_has_value && ipv4_is_default && ipv6_is_default) {
1881     // Both are defaults: dual-stack with default localhost addresses
1882     bind_ipv4 = true;
1883     bind_ipv6 = true;
1884     ipv4_address = "127.0.0.1";
1885     ipv6_address = "::1";
1886     log_info("Default dual-stack: binding to 127.0.0.1 (IPv4) and ::1 (IPv6)");
1887   } else if (ipv4_has_value && !ipv4_is_default && (!ipv6_has_value || ipv6_is_default)) {
1888     // IPv4 explicitly set, IPv6 is default or empty: bind only IPv4
1889     bind_ipv4 = true;
1890     bind_ipv6 = false;
1891     ipv4_address = GET_OPTION(address);
1892     log_info("Binding only to IPv4 address: %s", ipv4_address);
1893   } else if (ipv6_has_value && !ipv6_is_default && (ipv4_is_default || !ipv4_has_value)) {
1894     // IPv6 explicitly set, IPv4 is default or empty: bind only IPv6
1895     bind_ipv4 = false;
1896     bind_ipv6 = true;
1897     ipv6_address = GET_OPTION(address6);
1898     log_info("Binding only to IPv6 address: %s", ipv6_address);
1899   } else {
1900     // Both explicitly set or one explicit + one default: dual-stack
1901     bind_ipv4 = true;
1902     bind_ipv6 = true;
1903     ipv4_address = ipv4_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address) : "127.0.0.1";
1904     ipv6_address = ipv6_has_value ? GET_OPTION(address6) : "::1";
1905     log_info("Dual-stack binding: IPv4=%s, IPv6=%s", ipv4_address, ipv6_address);
1906   }
1907

1948   // Initialize TCP server (creates and binds sockets)
1949   memset(&g_tcp_server, 0, sizeof(g_tcp_server));

1951   if (tcp_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
1952     // Signal shutdown to allow threads to exit before we call FATAL

1954     log_debug("TCP server init failed, signaling shutdown to threads");
1955 
1956     // Clean up worker thread pool if it was created

1958       log_debug("Early cleanup: destroying server worker pool");
1959       thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
1960       g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
1961     }
1962 
1963     FATAL(ERROR_NETWORK, "Failed to initialize TCP server");
1964   }

1993   // =========================================================================
1994   // UPnP Port Mapping (Quick Win for Direct TCP)
1995   // =========================================================================
1996   // Track UPnP success for ACDS session type decision
1997   // If UPnP fails, we need to create a WebRTC session to enable client connectivity
1998   bool upnp_succeeded = false;
1999 
2000   // Try to open port via UPnP so direct TCP works for ~70% of home users.
2001   // If this fails, clients fall back to WebRTC automatically - not fatal.
2002   //
2003   // Strategy:
2004   //   1. UPnP (works on ~90% of home routers)
2005   //   2. NAT-PMP fallback (Apple routers)
2006   //   3. If both fail: use ACDS + WebRTC (reliable, but slightly higher latency)
2007   if (GET_OPTION(enable_upnp)) {
2008     asciichat_error_t upnp_result = nat_upnp_open(port, "ascii-chat Server", &g_upnp_ctx);
2009 
2010     if (upnp_result == ASCIICHAT_OK && g_upnp_ctx) {
2011       char public_addr[22];
2012       if (nat_upnp_get_address(g_upnp_ctx, public_addr, sizeof(public_addr)) == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2013         char msg[256];
2014         safe_snprintf(msg, sizeof(msg), "🌐 Public endpoint: %s (direct TCP)", public_addr);
2015         log_console(LOG_INFO, msg);
2016         log_info("UPnP: Port mapping successful, public endpoint: %s", public_addr);
2017         upnp_succeeded = true;
2018       }
2019     } else {
2020       log_info("UPnP: Port mapping unavailable or failed - will use WebRTC fallback");
2021       log_console(LOG_INFO, "📡 Clients behind strict NATs will use WebRTC fallback");
2022     }
2023   } else {
2024     log_debug("UPnP: Disabled (use --upnp to enable automatic port mapping)");
2025   }
2026 
2027   // Initialize synchronization primitives
2028   if (rwlock_init(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "clients") != 0) {
2029     FATAL(ERROR_THREAD, "Failed to initialize client manager rwlock");
2030   }
2031 
2032   // Register global sync primitives for debugging
2033   NAMED_REGISTER_RWLOCK(&g_client_manager_rwlock, "client_manager_rwlock", NULL);
2034 
2035   // Lock debug system already initialized earlier in main()
2036 
2037   // Check if SIGINT/SIGTERM was received during initialization
2038   // If so, skip the accept loop entirely and go to cleanup
2039   if (atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2040     log_info("Shutdown signal received during initialization, skipping server startup");
2041     goto cleanup;
2042   }
2043 
2044   // Lock debug thread already started earlier in main()
2045 
2046   // NOTE: g_client_manager is already zero-initialized in client.c with = {0}
2047   // We only need to initialize the mutex
2048   mutex_init(&g_client_manager.mutex, "client_manager");
2049 
2050   // Initialize uthash head pointer for O(1) lookup (uthash requires NULL initialization)
2051   g_client_manager.clients_by_id = NULL;
2052 
2053   // Initialize connection rate limiter (prevents DoS attacks)
2054   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2055     log_debug("Initializing connection rate limiter...");
2056     g_rate_limiter = rate_limiter_create_memory();
2057     if (!g_rate_limiter) {
2058       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize rate limiter");
2059       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2060         FATAL(ERROR_MEMORY, "Failed to create connection rate limiter");
2061       }
2062     } else {
2063       log_info("Connection rate limiter initialized (50 connections/min per IP)");
2064     }
2065   }
2066 
2067   // Initialize audio mixer (always enabled on server)
2068   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2069     log_debug("Initializing audio mixer for per-client audio rendering...");
2070     g_audio_mixer = mixer_create(MAX_CLIENTS, AUDIO_SAMPLE_RATE);
2071     if (!g_audio_mixer) {
2072       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize audio mixer");

2075       }
2076     } else {
2077       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2078         log_debug("Audio mixer initialized successfully for per-client audio rendering");
2079       }
2080     }
2081   }
2082 
2083   // Initialize H.265 codec server context (always enabled on server)
2084   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2085     log_debug("Initializing H.265 codec server context...");
2086     g_h265_server = h265_server_context_create();
2087     if (!g_h265_server) {
2088       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize H.265 codec context");

2092     } else {
2093       if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {

2095       }
2096     }
2097   }
2098 
2099   // Initialize mDNS context for LAN service discovery (optional)
2100   // mDNS allows clients on the LAN to discover this server without knowing its IP
2101   // Can be disabled with --no-mdns-advertise
2102   // Note: Actual advertisement is deferred until after ACDS session creation (if --acds is enabled)
2103   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit) && !GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2104     log_debug("Initializing mDNS for LAN service discovery...");
2105     g_mdns_ctx = asciichat_mdns_init();
2106     if (!g_mdns_ctx) {
2107       LOG_ERRNO_IF_SET("Failed to initialize mDNS (non-fatal, LAN discovery disabled)");
2108       log_warn("mDNS disabled - LAN service discovery will not be available");
2109       g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2110     } else {
2111       log_debug("mDNS context initialized, advertisement deferred until session string is ready");
2112     }
2113   } else if (GET_OPTION(no_mdns_advertise)) {
2114     log_info("mDNS service advertisement disabled via --no-mdns-advertise");
2115   }
2116 
2117   // ========================================================================
2118   // Session Host Creation (for discovery mode support)
2119   // ========================================================================
2120   // Create session_host to track clients in a transport-agnostic way.
2121   // This enables future discovery mode where participants can become hosts.
2122   if (!atomic_load_bool(&g_should_exit)) {
2123     session_host_config_t host_config = {
2124         .port = port,
2125         .ipv4_address = ipv4_address,
2126         .ipv6_address = ipv6_address,
2127         .max_clients = GET_OPTION(max_clients),
2128         .encryption_enabled = g_server_encryption_enabled,
2129         .key_path = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key),
2130         .password = GET_OPTION(password),
2131         .callbacks = {0}, // No callbacks for now
2132         .user_data = NULL,
2133     };
2134 
2135     server_ctx.session_host = session_host_create(&host_config);
2136     if (!server_ctx.session_host) {
2137       // Non-fatal: session_host is optional, server can work without it
2138       log_warn("Failed to create session_host (discovery mode support disabled)");
2139     } else {
2140       log_debug("Session host created for discovery mode support");
2141     }
2142   }
2143 
2144   // ========================================================================
2145   // MAIN CONNECTION LOOP - Delegated to tcp_server
2146   // ========================================================================
2147   //
2148   // The tcp_server module handles:
2149   // 1. Dual-stack IPv4/IPv6 accept loop with select() timeout
2150   // 2. Spawning client_handler threads for each connection
2151   // 3. Responsive shutdown when g_tcp_server.running is set to false
2152   //
2153   // Client lifecycle is managed by ascii_chat_client_handler() which:
2154   // - Performs rate limiting
2155   // - Calls add_client() to initialize structures and spawn workers
2156   // - Blocks until client disconnects
2157   // - Calls remove_client() to cleanup and stop worker threads
2158 
2159   // ACDS Session Creation: Register this server with discovery service
2160   // This also determines the session string for mDNS (if --acds is enabled)
2161   char session_string[64] = {0};
2162   bool session_is_mdns_only = false;
2163 
2164   // ACDS Registration (conditional on --discovery flag)
2165   if (GET_OPTION(discovery)) {
2166     // Security Requirement Check (Issue #239):
2167     // Server IP must be protected by password, identity verification, or explicit opt-in
2168 
2169     // Auto-detection: Check if password or identity verification is configured
2170     const char *password = GET_OPTION(password);
2171     bool has_password = password && strlen(password) > 0;
2172     const char *encrypt_key = GET_OPTION(encrypt_key);
2173     bool has_identity = encrypt_key && strlen(encrypt_key) > 0;
2174     bool explicit_expose = GET_OPTION(discovery_expose_ip) != 0;
2175 
2176     // Validate security configuration BEFORE attempting ACDS connection
2177     bool acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2178 
2179     if (has_password || has_identity) {
2180       // Auto-enable privacy: IP revealed only after verification
2181       acds_expose_ip_flag = false;
2182       log_plain("🔒 ACDS privacy enabled: IP disclosed only after %s verification",
2183                 has_password ? "password" : "identity");
2184     } else if (explicit_expose) {
2185       // Explicit opt-in to public IP disclosure
2186       // Only prompt if running interactively (can prompt user)
2187       // When non-interactive (automated/scripted), treat explicit flag as confirmation
2188       bool is_interactive = terminal_can_prompt_user();
2189 
2190       if (is_interactive) {
2191         log_warn("WARNING: You are about to allow PUBLIC IP disclosure!\n"
2192                  "Anyone with the session string will be able to see your IP address.\n"
2193                  "This is NOT RECOMMENDED unless you understand the privacy implications.");
2194 
2195         if (!platform_prompt_yes_no("Do you want to proceed with public IP disclosure", false)) {
2196           log_info("IP disclosure not confirmed. Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2197           goto skip_acds_session;
2198         }
2199       }
2200 
2201       // User confirmed (or running non-interactively with explicit flag) - proceed with public IP disclosure
2202       acds_expose_ip_flag = true;
2203       log_info("Public IP disclosure CONFIRMED.\n"
2204                "Your IP address will be visible to anyone with the session string");
2205     } else {
2206       // Security violation: No password, no identity, no explicit opt-in
2207       log_error("Cannot create ACDS session: No security configured!\n"
2208                 "You must either:\n"
2209                 "1. Set a password: --password \"your-secret\"\n"
2210                 "2. Use identity key: --key ~/.ssh/id_ed25519\n"
2211                 "3. Explicitly allow public IP: --acds-expose-ip (NOT RECOMMENDED)\n"
2212                 "\n"
2213                 "Server will run WITHOUT discovery service.");
2214       goto skip_acds_session;
2215     }
2216 
2217     // Security is configured, proceed with ACDS connection
2218     const char *acds_server = GET_OPTION(discovery_server);
2219     uint16_t acds_port = (uint16_t)GET_OPTION(discovery_port);
2220 
2221     log_info("Attempting to create session on ACDS server at %s:%d...", acds_server, acds_port);
2222 
2223     acds_client_config_t acds_config;
2224     acds_client_config_init_defaults(&acds_config);
2225     SAFE_STRNCPY(acds_config.server_address, acds_server, sizeof(acds_config.server_address));
2226     acds_config.server_port = acds_port;
2227     acds_config.timeout_ms = 5 * MS_PER_SEC_INT;
2228 
2229     // Allocate ACDS client on heap for server lifecycle
2230     g_acds_client = SAFE_MALLOC(sizeof(acds_client_t), acds_client_t *);
2231     if (!g_acds_client) {
2232       log_error("Failed to allocate ACDS client");
2233       goto skip_acds_session;
2234     }
2235 
2236     asciichat_error_t acds_connect_result = acds_client_connect(g_acds_client, &acds_config);
2237     if (acds_connect_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2238       log_error("Failed to connect to ACDS server at %s:%d: %s", acds_server, acds_port,
2239                 asciichat_error_string(acds_connect_result));
2240       goto skip_acds_session;
2241     }
2242     if (acds_connect_result == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2243       // Prepare session creation parameters
2244       acds_session_create_params_t create_params;
2245       memset(&create_params, 0, sizeof(create_params));
2246 
2247       // Use server's Ed25519 identity public key if available
2248       if (g_server_encryption_enabled && has_identity) {
2249         memcpy(create_params.identity_pubkey, g_server_private_key.public_key, 32);
2250         log_debug("Using server identity key for ACDS session");
2251       } else {
2252         // No identity key available - use zero key
2253         // ACDS will accept this if identity verification is not required
2254         memset(create_params.identity_pubkey, 0, 32);
2255         log_debug("No server identity key - using zero key for ACDS session");
2256       }
2257 
2258       create_params.capabilities = 0x03; // Video + Audio
2259       create_params.max_participants = GET_OPTION(max_clients);
2260       log_debug("ACDS: max_clients option value = %d", GET_OPTION(max_clients));
2261 
2262       // Set password if configured
2263       create_params.has_password = has_password;
2264       if (has_password) {
2265         // TODO: Hash password with Argon2id
2266         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.password, password, sizeof(create_params.password));
2267       }
2268 
2269       // Set IP disclosure policy (determined above)
2270       create_params.acds_expose_ip = acds_expose_ip_flag;
2271       log_info("DEBUG: Server setting acds_expose_ip=%d (explicit_expose=%d, has_password=%d, has_identity=%d)",
2272                create_params.acds_expose_ip, explicit_expose, has_password, has_identity);
2273 
2274       // Set session type (Direct TCP or WebRTC)
2275       // Auto-detect: Use WebRTC if UPnP failed OR if explicitly requested via --webrtc
2276       // Exception: If bind address is 0.0.0.0, server is on public IP - use Direct TCP
2277       const char *bind_addr = GET_OPTION(address);
2278       bool bind_all_interfaces = (strcmp(bind_addr, "0.0.0.0") == 0);
2279 
2280       // Determine session type: prefer WebRTC by default (unless explicitly disabled)
2281       // Priority: explicit --webrtc flag > connection type detection > UPnP > default
2282       if (GET_OPTION(webrtc)) {
2283         // Explicit WebRTC request
2284         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2285         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (explicitly requested via --webrtc)");
2286       } else if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2287         // Bind to 0.0.0.0: use WebRTC as default (better NAT compatibility)
2288         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2289         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (default for 0.0.0.0 binding, provides NAT-agnostic connections)");
2290       } else if (upnp_succeeded) {
2291         // UPnP port mapping worked - can use direct TCP
2292         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_DIRECT_TCP;
2293         log_info("ACDS session type: Direct TCP (UPnP succeeded, server is publicly accessible)");
2294       } else {
2295         // UPnP failed and not on public IP - use WebRTC for NAT traversal
2296         create_params.session_type = SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC;
2297         log_info("ACDS session type: WebRTC (UPnP failed, server behind NAT)");
2298       }
2299 
2300       // Server connection information (where clients should connect)
2301       // If bind address is 0.0.0.0, leave server_address empty for ACDS to auto-detect public IP
2302       if (bind_all_interfaces) {
2303         create_params.server_address[0] = '\0'; // Empty - ACDS will use connection source IP
2304         log_debug("Bind address is 0.0.0.0, ACDS will auto-detect public IP from connection");
2305       } else {
2306         SAFE_STRNCPY(create_params.server_address, bind_addr, sizeof(create_params.server_address));
2307       }
2308       create_params.server_port = port;
2309 
2310       // DEBUG: Log what we're sending to ACDS
2311       log_info("DEBUG: Before SESSION_CREATE - expose_ip_publicly=%d, server_address='%s' port=%u, session_type=%u",
2312                create_params.acds_expose_ip, create_params.server_address, create_params.server_port,
2313                create_params.session_type);
2314 
2315       // Create session
2316       acds_session_create_result_t create_result;
2317       asciichat_error_t create_err = acds_session_create(g_acds_client, &create_params, &create_result);
2318 
2319       if (create_err == ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2320         SAFE_STRNCPY(session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(session_string));
2321         SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, create_result.session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2322         session_is_mdns_only = false; // Session is now registered with ACDS (globally discoverable)
2323         log_info("Session created: %s", session_string);
2324 
2325         // Server must join its own session so ACDS can route signaling messages
2326         log_debug("Server joining session as first participant for WebRTC signaling...");
2327         acds_session_join_params_t join_params = {0};
2328         join_params.session_string = session_string;
2329 
2330         // Use same identity key as session creation
2331         memcpy(join_params.identity_pubkey, create_params.identity_pubkey, 32);
2332 
2333         // Include password if session is password-protected
2334         if (has_password) {
2335           join_params.has_password = true;
2336           SAFE_STRNCPY(join_params.password, password, sizeof(join_params.password));
2337         }
2338 
2339         acds_session_join_result_t join_result = {0};
2340         asciichat_error_t join_err = acds_session_join(g_acds_client, &join_params, &join_result);
2341         if (join_err != ASCIICHAT_OK || !join_result.success) {
2342           log_error("Failed to join own session: %s (error: %s)", asciichat_error_string(join_err),
2343                     join_result.error_message[0] ? join_result.error_message : "unknown");
2344           // Continue anyway - this is not fatal for Direct TCP sessions
2345         } else {
2346           log_debug("Server joined session successfully (participant_id: %02x%02x...)", join_result.participant_id[0],
2347                     join_result.participant_id[1]);
2348           // Store participant ID for WebRTC signaling (needed to identify server in SDP/ICE messages)
2349           memcpy(g_server_participant_id, join_result.participant_id, 16);
2350           log_debug("Stored server participant_id for signaling: %02x%02x...", g_server_participant_id[0],
2351                     g_server_participant_id[1]);
2352           memcpy(create_result.session_id, join_result.session_id, 16);
2353         }
2354 
2355         // Keep ACDS connection alive for WebRTC signaling relay
2356         log_debug("Server staying connected to ACDS for signaling relay");
2357 
2358         // Create ACDS transport wrapper for sending signaling packets
2359         g_acds_transport = acip_tcp_transport_create("transport_acds_server", g_acds_client->socket, NULL);
2360         if (!g_acds_transport) {
2361           log_error("Failed to create ACDS transport wrapper");
2362         } else {
2363           log_debug("ACDS transport wrapper created for signaling");
2364 
2365           // Start ACDS ping thread to keep connection alive (for ALL session types)
2366           int ping_thread_result = asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_ping_thread, "acds_ping", acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2367           if (ping_thread_result != 0) {
2368             log_error("Failed to create ACDS ping thread: %d", ping_thread_result);
2369           } else {
2370             log_debug("ACDS ping thread started to keep connection alive");
2371             g_acds_ping_thread_started = true;
2372           }
2373         }
2374 
2375         // Initialize WebRTC peer_manager if session type is WebRTC
2376         if (create_params.session_type == SESSION_TYPE_WEBRTC) {
2377           log_debug("Initializing WebRTC library and peer manager for session (role=CREATOR)...");
2378 
2379           // Initialize WebRTC library (libdatachannel)
2380           asciichat_error_t webrtc_init_result = webrtc_init();
2381           if (webrtc_init_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2382             log_error("Failed to initialize WebRTC library: %s", asciichat_error_string(webrtc_init_result));
2383             g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2384           } else {
2385             log_debug("WebRTC library initialized successfully");
2386 
2387             // Configure STUN servers for ICE gathering (static to persist for peer_manager lifetime)
2388             static stun_server_t stun_servers[4] = {0};
2389             static lifecycle_t g_stun_lc = LIFECYCLE_INIT;
2390             static int stun_count = 0; // Store actual count separately
2391 
2392             if (lifecycle_init(&g_stun_lc, "stun")) {
2393               log_debug("Parsing STUN servers from options: '%s'", GET_OPTION(stun_servers));
2394               int count =
2395                   stun_servers_parse(GET_OPTION(stun_servers), OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, stun_servers, 4);
2396               if (count > 0) {
2397                 stun_count = count;
2398                 log_debug("Parsed %d STUN servers", count);
2399                 for (int i = 0; i < count; i++) {
2400                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2401                 }
2402               } else {
2403                 log_warn("Failed to parse STUN servers, using defaults");
2404                 stun_count = stun_servers_parse(OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT, OPT_ENDPOINT_STUN_SERVERS_DEFAULT,
2405                                                 stun_servers, 4);
2406                 log_debug("Using default STUN servers, count=%d", stun_count);
2407                 for (int i = 0; i < stun_count; i++) {
2408                   log_debug("  STUN[%d]: '%s' (len=%d)", i, stun_servers[i].host, stun_servers[i].host_len);
2409                 }
2410               }
2411             }
2412 
2413             // Configure peer_manager
2414             webrtc_peer_manager_config_t pm_config = {
2415                 .role = WEBRTC_ROLE_CREATOR, // Server accepts offers, generates answers
2416                 .stun_servers = stun_servers,
2417                 .stun_count = stun_count,
2418                 .turn_servers = NULL, // No TURN for server (clients should have public IP or use TURN)
2419                 .turn_count = 0,
2420                 .on_transport_ready = on_webrtc_transport_ready,
2421                 .user_data = &server_ctx,
2422                 .crypto_ctx = NULL // WebRTC handles crypto internally
2423             };
2424 
2425             // Configure signaling callbacks for relaying SDP/ICE via ACDS
2426             webrtc_signaling_callbacks_t signaling_callbacks = {
2427                 .send_sdp = server_send_sdp, .send_ice = server_send_ice, .user_data = NULL};
2428 
2429             // Create peer_manager
2430             asciichat_error_t pm_result =
2431                 webrtc_peer_manager_create(&pm_config, &signaling_callbacks, &g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2432             if (pm_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2433               log_error("Failed to create WebRTC peer_manager: %s", asciichat_error_string(pm_result));
2434               g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2435             } else {
2436               log_debug("WebRTC peer_manager initialized successfully");
2437 
2438               // Start ACDS receive thread for WebRTC signaling relay
2439               int thread_result =
2440                   asciichat_thread_create(&g_acds_receive_thread, "acds_recv", acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2441               if (thread_result != 0) {
2442                 log_error("Failed to create ACDS receive thread: %d", thread_result);
2443                 // Cleanup peer_manager since signaling won't work
2444                 webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2445                 g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2446               } else {
2447                 log_debug("ACDS receive thread started for WebRTC signaling relay");
2448                 g_acds_receive_thread_started = true;
2449               }
2450             }
2451           } // Close else block from webrtc_init() success
2452         } else {
2453           log_debug("Session type is DIRECT_TCP, skipping WebRTC peer_manager initialization");
2454         }
2455 
2456         // Advertise mDNS with ACDS session string
2457         // This ensures both mDNS and ACDS discovery return the same session string
2458         advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2459       } else {
2460         log_warn("Failed to create session on ACDS server (server will run without discovery)");
2461         // Clean up failed ACDS client
2462         if (g_acds_client) {
2463           acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2464           SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2465           g_acds_client = NULL;
2466         }
2467       }
2468     } else {
2469       log_warn("Could not connect to ACDS server at %s:%d (server will run without discovery)", acds_server, acds_port);
2470     }
2471   } else {
2472     log_info("ACDS registration disabled (use --acds to enable)");
2473   }
2474 
2475 skip_acds_session:
2476   // Fallback: If no session string was set by ACDS (either disabled or failed),
2477   // generate a random session string for mDNS discovery only
2478   if (session_string[0] == '\0' && g_mdns_ctx) {
2479     log_debug("No ACDS session string available, generating random session for mDNS");
2480 
2481     // Use the proper session string generation from discovery module
2482     // This generates adjective-noun-noun format using the full wordlists
2483     if (acds_string_generate(session_string, sizeof(session_string)) != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2484       log_error("Failed to generate session string for mDNS");
2485       return 1;
2486     }
2487 
2488     log_debug("Generated random session string for mDNS: '%s'", session_string);
2489 
2490     // Mark that this session is mDNS-only (not globally discoverable via ACDS)
2491     session_is_mdns_only = true;
2492 
2493     // Advertise mDNS with random session string
2494     advertise_mdns_with_session(session_string, (uint16_t)port);
2495   }
2496 
2497   // ====================================================================
2498   // Display session string prominently as the FINAL startup message
2499   // This ensures users see the connection info clearly without logs
2500   // wiping it away
2501   // ====================================================================
2502   if (session_string[0] != '\0') {
2503     if (session_is_mdns_only) {
2504       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s (LAN only via "
2505                 "mDNS)\n🔗 Share with others on your LAN to join:\n   ascii-chat "
2506                 "%s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2507                 session_string, session_string);
2508     } else {
2509       log_plain("━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━\n📋 Session String: %s\n🔗 Share this "
2510                 "globally to join:\n   ascii-chat %s\n━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━━",
2511                 session_string, session_string);
2512     }
2513   }
2514 
2515   // Copy session info to globals for status screen display
2516   SAFE_STRNCPY(g_session_string, session_string, sizeof(g_session_string));
2517   g_session_is_mdns_only = session_is_mdns_only;
2518 
2519   log_debug("Server entering accept loop (port %d)...", port);
2520 
2521   // Initialize status screen
2522   g_server_start_time = time(NULL);
2523   g_last_status_update = platform_get_monotonic_time_us();
2524 
2525   // Clear status screen log buffer to discard initialization logs
2526   // This ensures only NEW logs (generated after status screen starts) are displayed
2527   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2528     ui_status_log_clear();
2529   }
2530 
2531   // Start status screen thread if enabled
2532   // Runs independently at target FPS (default 60 Hz), decoupled from network accept loop
2533   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2534     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_status_screen_thread, "status_screen", status_screen_thread, NULL) != 0) {
2535       log_error("Failed to create status screen thread");
2536       goto cleanup;
2537     }
2538     log_debug("Status screen thread started");
2539   }
2540 
2541   // Start WebSocket server thread if initialized
2542   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2543     if (asciichat_thread_create(&g_websocket_server_thread, "ws_server", websocket_server_thread_wrapper,
2544                                 &g_websocket_server) != 0) {
2545       log_error("Failed to create WebSocket server thread");
2546     } else {
2547       g_websocket_server_thread_started = true;
2548       log_info("WebSocket server thread started");
2549     }
2550   }
2551 
2552   // Run TCP server (blocks until shutdown signal received)
2553   // tcp_server_run() handles:
2554   // - select() on IPv4/IPv6 sockets with timeout
2555   // - accept() new connections
2556   // - Spawn ascii_chat_client_handler() thread for each connection
2557   // - Responsive shutdown when atomic_store_bool(&g_tcp_server.running, false)
2558   asciichat_error_t run_result = tcp_server_run(&g_tcp_server);
2559   if (run_result != ASCIICHAT_OK) {
2560     log_error("TCP server exited with error");
2561   }
2562 
2563   log_debug("Server accept loop exited");
2564 
2565 cleanup:
2566   // Signal status screen thread to exit
2567   atomic_store_bool(&g_should_exit, true);
2568 
2569   // Wait for status screen thread to finish if it was started
2570   if (GET_OPTION(status_screen)) {
2571     log_debug("Waiting for status screen thread to exit...");
2572     int join_result = asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_status_screen_thread, NULL, NS_PER_SEC_INT); // 1 second
2573     if (join_result != 0) {
2574       log_warn("Status screen thread did not exit cleanly (timeout)");
2575     } else {
2576       log_debug("Status screen thread exited");
2577     }
2578   }
2579 
2580   // Cleanup
2581   log_debug("Server shutting down...");
2582   memset(g_session_string, 0, sizeof(g_session_string)); // Clear session string for status screen
2583 
2584   // Wake up any threads that might be blocked on condition variables
2585   // (like packet queues) to ensure responsive shutdown
2586   // This must happen BEFORE client cleanup to wake up any blocked threads
2587   static_cond_broadcast(&g_shutdown_cond);
2588   // NOTE: Do NOT call cond_destroy() on statically-initialized condition variables
2589   // g_shutdown_cond uses STATIC_COND_INIT which doesn't allocate resources that need cleanup
2590   // Calling cond_destroy() on a static cond is undefined behavior on some platforms
2591 
2592   // Close all client sockets immediately to unblock receive threads.
2593   // The signal handler only closed the listening socket, but client receive threads
2594   // are still blocked in recv_with_timeout(). We need to close their sockets to unblock them.
2595   log_debug("Closing all client sockets to unblock receive threads...");
2596 
2597   // Use write lock since we're modifying client->socket
2598   rwlock_wrlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2599   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2600     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2601     if (client->client_id[0] != '\0' && client->socket != INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE) {
2602       socket_close(client->socket);
2603       client->socket = INVALID_SOCKET_VALUE;
2604     }
2605   }
2606   rwlock_wrunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2607 
2608   log_debug("Signaling all clients to stop (sockets closed, g_should_exit set)...");
2609 
2610   // Stop and destroy server worker thread pool (stats logger, etc.)
2611   if (g_server_worker_pool) {
2612     thread_pool_destroy(g_server_worker_pool);
2613     g_server_worker_pool = NULL;
2614     log_debug("Server worker thread pool stopped");
2615   }
2616 
2617   // Destroy rate limiter
2618   if (g_rate_limiter) {
2619     rate_limiter_destroy(g_rate_limiter);
2620     g_rate_limiter = NULL;
2621   }
2622 
2623   // Shutdown WebSocket server BEFORE removing clients.
2624   // The LWS event thread runs callbacks that access transport data (impl_data).
2625   // If we destroy transports via remove_client while LWS is still running,
2626   // callbacks access freed memory (heap-use-after-free).
2627   // lws_context_destroy fires LWS_CALLBACK_CLOSED for all connections, which
2628   // closes transports and NULLs the per-session conn_data->transport pointer.
2629   // After this, remove_client can safely destroy the transport objects.
2630   bool websocket_exited_cleanly = true;  // Default to clean unless timeout detected
2631   if (g_websocket_server.context != NULL) {
2632     log_debug("Shutting down WebSocket server before client cleanup");
2633     atomic_store_u64(&g_websocket_server.running, false);
2634     // Wake the LWS event loop immediately instead of waiting for the 50ms
2635     // lws_service timeout. This is safe because the event loop thread can't
2636     // destroy the context until after it exits its loop (which requires
2637     // seeing running=false, which we just set).
2638     websocket_server_cancel_service(&g_websocket_server);
2639 
2640     // Join with 500ms timeout. If the WebSocket thread is deadlocked in lws_service(),
2641     // it won't exit and we skip the graceful handler thread cleanup to avoid prolonged
2642     // shutdown hangs. Forceful cleanup will happen in websocket_server_destroy().
2643     if (g_websocket_server_thread_started) {
2644       int join_result =
2645           asciichat_thread_join_timeout(&g_websocket_server_thread, NULL, 500 * NS_PER_MS_INT); // 500ms in ns
2646       if (join_result != 0) {
2647         log_warn("WebSocket thread did not exit within 500ms, skipping graceful handler cleanup");
2648         websocket_exited_cleanly = false;
2649       }
2650       g_websocket_server_thread_started = false;
2651     }
2652 
2653     if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2654       log_debug("WebSocket thread confirmed exited, waiting for handler threads to complete...");
2655     }
2656   }
2657 
2658   // Wait for all client handler threads to exit BEFORE destroying WebSocket context
2659   // Handler threads may still be working with WebSocket connections, so we must
2660   // ensure they complete before destroying the context (which would abort their operations).
2661   // Skip this if WebSocket thread didn't exit cleanly (deadlock detected).
2662   if (websocket_exited_cleanly) {
2663     int retry_count = 0;
2664     while (retry_count < 50) { // Max 500ms wait
2665       rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2666       bool all_cleaned = true;
2667       for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2668         if (g_client_manager.clients[i].client_id[0] != '\0') {
2669           all_cleaned = false;
2670           break;
2671         }
2672       }
2673       rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2674 
2675       if (all_cleaned)
2676         break;
2677       platform_sleep_us(10 * US_PER_MS_INT); // 10ms
2678       retry_count++;
2679     }
2680     log_debug("Client handler threads cleaned up, destroying WebSocket server");
2681   } else {
2682     log_warn("Skipping graceful handler thread cleanup due to WebSocket deadlock");
2683   }
2684 
2685   // ★ CRITICAL: Cleanup debug sync BEFORE destroying websocket_server
2686   // The debug_sync thread runs concurrently and accesses the named registry.
2687   // If websocket_server_destroy() frees thread pool memory first, the debug thread
2688   // will get a use-after-free error trying to check condition variables.
2689   // Moving this before websocket destroy prevents the race condition.
2690 #ifndef NDEBUG
2691   debug_sync_destroy();
2692 #endif
2693 
2694   // Now destroy WebSocket server after all handler threads have completed
2695   // This ensures no handler threads are still accessing the context.
2696   // (It's safe to call even if context was already destroyed forcefully above)
2697   websocket_server_destroy(&g_websocket_server);
2698   log_debug("WebSocket server destroyed");
2699 
2700   // Cleanup status screen log capture (must be AFTER all client threads exit to avoid use-after-free)
2701   // Client threads may still be appending logs during the wait loop above, so we destroy the buffer only
2702   // after we've confirmed they've all exited.
2703   ui_status_log_destroy();
2704 
2705   // Clean up all connected clients
2706   log_debug("Cleaning up connected clients...");
2707   // FIXED: Simplified to collect client IDs first, then remove them without holding locks
2708   char clients_to_remove[MAX_CLIENTS][MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN];
2709   int client_count = 0;
2710 
2711   rwlock_rdlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2712   for (int i = 0; i < MAX_CLIENTS; i++) {
2713     client_info_t *client = &g_client_manager.clients[i];
2714 
2715     // Only attempt to clean up clients that were actually connected
2716     // (client_id is empty string for uninitialized clients)
2717     // FIXED: Only access mutex for initialized clients to avoid accessing uninitialized mutex
2718     if (client->client_id[0] == '\0') {
2719       continue; // Skip uninitialized clients
2720     }
2721 
2722     // Use snapshot pattern to avoid holding both locks simultaneously
2723     // This prevents deadlock by not acquiring client_state_mutex while holding rwlock
2724     SAFE_STRNCPY(clients_to_remove[client_count], client->client_id, MAX_CLIENT_ID_LEN - 1);
2725 
2726     // Clean up ANY client that was allocated, whether active or not
2727     // (disconnected clients may not be active but still have resources)
2728     client_count++;
2729   }
2730   rwlock_rdunlock(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2731 
2732   // Remove all clients without holding any locks
2733   for (int i = 0; i < client_count; i++) {
2734     if (remove_client(&server_ctx, clients_to_remove[i]) != 0) {
2735       log_error("Failed to remove client %s during shutdown", clients_to_remove[i]);
2736     }
2737   }
2738 
2739   // Clean up hash table
2740   // Cannot use HASH_ITER when deleting - HASH_DELETE modifies the table and can set
2741   // the pointer to NULL, causing HASH_ITER to dereference a null pointer.
2742   // Instead, repeatedly delete the first item until the table is empty.
2743   while (g_client_manager.clients_by_id) {
2744     client_info_t *current_client = g_client_manager.clients_by_id;
2745     HASH_DELETE(hh, g_client_manager.clients_by_id, current_client);
2746     // Note: We don't free current_client here because it's part of the clients[] array
2747   }
2748 
2749   // Clean up audio mixer
2750   if (g_audio_mixer) {
2751     // Set to NULL first before destroying.
2752     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_audio_mixer.
2753     // Setting it to NULL first prevents use-after-free race condition.
2754     // volatile ensures this write is visible to other threads immediately
2755     mixer_t *mixer_to_destroy = g_audio_mixer;
2756     g_audio_mixer = NULL;
2757     mixer_destroy(mixer_to_destroy);
2758   }
2759 
2760   // Clean up H.265 codec server context
2761   if (g_h265_server) {
2762     // Set to NULL first before destroying, similar to g_audio_mixer
2763     // Client handler threads may still be running and checking g_h265_server.
2764     h265_server_context_t *h265_to_destroy = g_h265_server;
2765     g_h265_server = NULL;
2766     h265_server_context_destroy(h265_to_destroy);
2767     log_debug("H.265 codec server context shut down");
2768   }
2769 
2770   // Clean up mDNS context
2771   if (g_mdns_ctx) {
2772     asciichat_mdns_destroy(g_mdns_ctx);
2773     g_mdns_ctx = NULL;
2774     log_debug("mDNS context shut down");
2775   }
2776 
2777   // Clean up synchronization primitives
2778   rwlock_destroy(&g_client_manager_rwlock);
2779   mutex_destroy(&g_client_manager.mutex);
2780 
2781 #ifndef NDEBUG
2782   // Clean up statistics system
2783   stats_cleanup();
2784 
2785   // Note: debug_sync_destroy() is now called earlier (before websocket_server_destroy)
2786   // to avoid use-after-free race condition with the debug monitoring thread
2787 #endif
2788 
2789   // Destroy session host (before TCP server shutdown)
2790   if (server_ctx.session_host) {
2791     log_debug("Destroying session host");
2792     session_host_destroy(server_ctx.session_host);
2793     server_ctx.session_host = NULL;
2794   }
2795 
2796   // Destroy H.265 server codec context
2797   if (server_ctx.h265_server) {
2798     log_debug("Destroying H.265 server context");
2799     h265_server_context_destroy(server_ctx.h265_server);
2800     server_ctx.h265_server = NULL;
2801   }
2802 
2803   // Shutdown TCP server (closes listen sockets and cleans up)
2804   tcp_server_destroy(&g_tcp_server);
2805 
2806   // WebSocket server already shut down before client cleanup (above).
2807 
2808   // Join ACDS threads (if started)
2809   // NOTE: Must be done BEFORE destroying transport to ensure clean shutdown
2810   if (g_acds_ping_thread_started) {
2811     log_debug("Joining ACDS ping thread");
2812     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_ping_thread, NULL);
2813     g_acds_ping_thread_started = false;
2814     log_debug("ACDS ping thread joined");
2815   }
2816 
2817   if (g_acds_receive_thread_started) {
2818     log_debug("Joining ACDS receive thread");
2819     asciichat_thread_join(&g_acds_receive_thread, NULL);
2820     g_acds_receive_thread_started = false;
2821     log_debug("ACDS receive thread joined");
2822   }
2823 
2824   // Clean up WebRTC peer manager (if initialized for ACDS signaling relay)
2825   if (g_webrtc_peer_manager) {
2826     log_debug("Destroying WebRTC peer manager");
2827     webrtc_peer_manager_destroy(g_webrtc_peer_manager);
2828     g_webrtc_peer_manager = NULL;
2829   }
2830 
2831   // Clean up ACDS transport wrapper (if created)
2832   if (g_acds_transport) {
2833     log_debug("Destroying ACDS transport wrapper");
2834     acip_transport_destroy(g_acds_transport);
2835     g_acds_transport = NULL;
2836   }
2837 
2838   // Disconnect from ACDS server (if connected for WebRTC signaling relay)
2839   if (g_acds_client) {
2840     log_debug("Disconnecting from ACDS server");
2841     acds_client_disconnect(g_acds_client);
2842     SAFE_FREE(g_acds_client);
2843     g_acds_client = NULL;
2844   }
2845   // Disable keepawake mode (re-allow OS to sleep)
2846   platform_disable_keepawake();
2847 
2848   // Clean up platform-specific resources (Windows: Winsock cleanup, timer restoration)
2849   // POSIX: minimal cleanup (symbol cache already handled above on Windows)
2850   socket_cleanup();
2851 
2852   log_info("Server shutdown complete");
2853 
2854   asciichat_error_stats_print();
2855 
2856   // Use exit() to allow atexit() handlers to run
2857   // Cleanup functions are idempotent (check if initialized first)
2858   return 0;
2859 }

Variable
  Documentation




mixer_t* volatile g_audio_mixer = NULL


Global audio mixer instance for multi-client audio processing.
    Global audio mixer.




The mixer combines audio streams from multiple clients, excluding
    each client's own audio from their outbound stream (preventing echo).
    Created once during server initialization and shared by all audio render
    threads.




THREAD SAFETY: The mixer itself is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently by multiple render.c audio threads without external
    synchronization. During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
    use-after-free.


Definition at line 173 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by add_client(), add_webrtc_client(),
    client_audio_render_thread(), remove_client(), and
    server_main().






public_key_t g_client_whitelist[MAX_CLIENTS] =
  {0}


Global client public key whitelist. Whitelist of allowed client
    public keys.




Array of public keys for clients that are authorized to connect to
    the server. Used for client authentication when whitelist mode is enabled.
    Sized to hold up to MAX_CLIENTS entries.




Note


Only used when client authentication is enabled



Accessed from crypto.c for client authentication




Definition at line 523 of file server/main.c.


523 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






h265_server_context_t* volatile g_h265_server = NULL


Global H.265 codec server context. Shared H.265 decoding context
    used by all packet handlers to decode client H.265 frames. Each client gets
    its own decoder instance.




THREAD SAFETY: The h265_server context is thread-safe and can be
    used concurrently by multiple client handlers. Decoders are per-client.
    During shutdown, set to NULL before destroying to prevent
  use-after-free.


Definition at line 185 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by handle_image_frame_h265_packet(), and
    server_main().






size_t
  g_num_server_identity_keys = 0


Number of loaded server identity keys. Number of server identity
    keys in use.




Tracks how many identity keys were successfully loaded from --key
    flags. Zero means server is running in simple mode (no identity key).


Definition at line 510 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






size_t
  g_num_whitelisted_clients = 0


Number of whitelisted clients. Tracks the current number of
    entries in g_client_whitelist that are valid and active. Used to iterate the
    whitelist and check authorization.


Definition at line 533 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by server_crypto_handshake(), and
    server_main().






rate_limiter_t* g_rate_limiter = NULL


Global rate limiter for connection attempts and packet processing.
    Global connection rate limiter.




In-memory rate limiter to prevent connection flooding and DoS
    attacks. Tracks connection attempts and packet rates per IP address with
    configurable limits.




Default limits (from rate_limit.c):


    
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONNECTION: 50 connections per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_IMAGE_FRAME: 144 FPS (8640 frames/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_AUDIO: 172 FPS (10320 packets/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_PING: 2 Hz (120 pings/min)
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CLIENT_JOIN: 10 joins per 60 seconds
    • 
  
  • RATE_EVENT_CONTROL: 100 control packets per 60 seconds
    • 



THREAD SAFETY: The rate limiter is thread-safe and can be used
    concurrently from the main accept loop and packet handlers without external
    synchronization.




See also


main.h for extern declaration


Definition at line 215 of file server/main.c.


Referenced by process_decrypted_packet(), and
    server_main().






bool g_server_encryption_enabled = false


Global flag indicating if server encryption is enabled. Server
    encryption enabled flag.




Set to true when the server is configured to use encryption and
    has successfully loaded a private key. Controls whether the server performs
    cryptographic handshakes with clients.




Note


Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto
  operations

private_key_t
  g_server_identity_keys[MAX_IDENTITY_KEYS] = {0}


Global server identity keys array (multi-key support). Array of
    server identity keys.




Stores all server identity keys loaded from multiple --key flags.
    Enables servers to present different keys (SSH, GPG) based on client
    expectations. Server selects the appropriate key during handshake based on
    what the client downloaded from ACDS.




Note


g_server_private_key points to g_server_identity_keys[0]
  for compatibility


Definition at line 500 of file server/main.c.


500 {0};






Referenced by server_crypto_handshake().






private_key_t g_server_private_key = {0}


Global server private key (first identity key, for backward
    compatibility). Server's private key.




Stores the server's primary private key loaded from the first
    --key flag. Used for cryptographic handshakes and packet
    encryption/decryption. Initialized during server startup from the configured
    key file path.




Note


This is an alias to g_server_identity_keys[0] for
  backward compatibility



Accessed from crypto.c for server-side crypto operations

static_cond_t g_shutdown_cond =
  STATIC_COND_INIT


Global shutdown condition variable for waking blocked threads.
    Used to wake up threads that might be blocked on condition variables (like
    packet queues) during shutdown. This ensures responsive shutdown even when
    threads are waiting on blocking operations.
